about.heroDesc: Random but valid phone number generator for countries worldwide
about.whatDesc1: PhoneGen is an online service for generating valid phone numbers for various countries. The service is useful for developers, testers, and analysts who need realistic test data.
about.whatDesc2: All numbers are generated following real country formats and can be used for phone validation testing, form filling, demonstrations, and other purposes.
about.features_35plus_desc: Support for phone numbers from more than 35 countries worldwide
about.features_3formats_desc: International, national, and E.164 number formats
about.features_valid_desc: All numbers are generated according to real country standards
about.features_free_desc: Completely free service for all users
about.faq_q1: Can I use these numbers for real purposes?
about.faq_a1: No, these are generated numbers. They don't belong to anyone and cannot be used for sending real messages or making calls.
about.faq_a2: Numbers are generated according to real standards and formats of each country. They look like real numbers but are actually random combinations.
about.faq_a3: No, the service is completely free and without restrictions. Use it as you wish.
about.faq_a4: On the main page, select your country and format, then click the 'Copy' button next to the desired number.
about.ctaDesc: Go to the main page and start generating valid numbers right now

productLanding.whatCanYouGenerateDesc: Choose a phone number type to generate
productLanding.ctaDesc: Go to the generator and start creating realistic data right now
productLanding.popularCountriesDesc: Start with one of the most popular countries

phone.heroDesc: Generate valid phone numbers for 245+ countries and regions worldwide. All numbers pass Google's libphonenumber-js validation and support multiple formatting options.

mobile.label: Mobile Numbers
mobile.desc: Generate mobile phone numbers for any country. Supports all major carriers and number formats.

fixedLine.label: Fixed Line Numbers
fixedLine.desc: Generate landline numbers with area codes and local subscriber numbers.

tollFree.label: Toll-Free Numbers
tollFree.desc: Generate toll-free numbers including 800, 888, 877, and similar prefixes.

voip.label: VoIP Numbers
voip.desc: Generate VoIP numbers for internet-based telephony services.

phone.validNumbersDesc: All generated numbers pass Google's libphonenumber-js validation for maximum realism.
phone.multipleFormatsDesc: Supports international, national, E.164, and RFC 3966 formatting options.
phone.allCountriesDesc: Every country and region with an assigned ITU country code is supported.
phone.freeUnlimitedDesc: No sign-up, no limits, no ads. Generate as many numbers as you need.
phone.faq_q1: Can I use the generated numbers for real calls or SMS?
phone.faq_a1: No, these numbers are randomly generated and do not belong to any real person or service. They cannot receive calls or messages.
phone.faq_q2: How realistic are the generated numbers?
phone.faq_a2: Very realistic. Numbers follow each country's actual numbering plan, including valid area codes, carrier prefixes, and correct digit lengths.
phone.faq_q3: Is there a limit on how many numbers I can generate?
phone.faq_a3: No limits. Generate as many numbers as you need, in any format, for any supported country.

user.heroDesc: Generate realistic user profiles with names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, and more. Perfect for testing registration systems and populating demo data.
user.feature1Desc: Generate full user profiles including name, email, phone, address, company, and password.
user.feature2Desc: Generate users from multiple countries with locale-appropriate names and addresses.
user.feature3Desc: Copy profiles individually or export in bulk as TXT, CSV, or JSON.
user.feature4Desc: No sign-up, no limits. Generate as many user profiles as you need.
user.faq_q1: Are the generated users based on real people?
user.faq_a1: No. All user profiles are randomly generated and do not correspond to any real person.
user.faq_q2: Can I customize the generated data?
user.faq_a2: Yes, you can configure which fields to include and select the country for locale-appropriate data.
user.faq_q3: Is user data stored on your server?
user.faq_a3: No. All generation happens entirely in your browser. Nothing is sent to or stored on any server.

address.heroDesc: Generate realistic addresses for 20+ countries. Includes street, city, postal code, region, and full address formatting.
address.feature1Desc: Addresses follow real postal formats for each country, including local street naming conventions.
address.feature2Desc: Support for 20+ countries with locale-appropriate address structures and postal codes.
address.feature3Desc: Complete addresses with street, city, state/region, postal code, and country.
address.feature4Desc: No sign-up or limits. Generate addresses instantly in your browser.
address.faq_q1: How accurate are the generated addresses?
address.faq_a1: Addresses follow each country's postal format but are randomly generated and do not correspond to real locations.
address.faq_a2: We support 20+ countries including US, UK, Germany, France, Russia, China, Japan, Brazil, and more.
address.faq_q3: Can I use these addresses for shipping?
address.faq_a3: No. These addresses are randomly generated and should only be used for testing and development purposes.

email.heroDesc: Generate realistic email addresses in 5 modes: random, professional, corporate, disposable, and nickname-based. Perfect for testing registration and form validation.
email.feature1Desc: Random, professional, corporate, disposable, and nickname modes for different use cases.
email.feature2Desc: All email addresses follow RFC 5322 format with valid domains and TLDs.
email.feature3Desc: Configure name format, domain type, and other options to match your needs.
email.feature4Desc: Generate unlimited email addresses instantly. No sign-up required.
email.faq_a1: No. Generated email addresses are random and do not have real mailboxes.
email.faq_a2: Domains are randomly selected from a pool of common email providers and custom-style domains.
email.faq_q3: Can I use these for testing email validation?
email.faq_a3: Yes. The generated emails follow proper format and can be used to test client-side and server-side validation.

username.heroDesc: Generate unique usernames in 7 styles: classic, modern, developer, gaming, professional, corporate, and random. Perfect for testing authentication and registration systems.
username.feature1Desc: Classic, modern, developer, gaming, professional, corporate, and random styles.
username.feature2Desc: Adjust length, add numbers, separators, and control casing to match your needs.
username.feature3Desc: Each username is randomly generated for maximum variety and uniqueness.
username.feature4Desc: Generate unlimited usernames instantly with no sign-up required.
username.faq_q1: Can I control the username format?
username.faq_a1: Yes. You can choose from 7 styles and configure length, numbers, separators, and casing.
username.faq_a2: Each generation produces random results. For guaranteed uniqueness across sessions, use the seed feature.
username.faq_q3: Can I use these for testing?
username.faq_a3: Yes. Generated usernames are perfect for testing registration forms, authentication systems, and database seeding.

company.heroDesc: Generate realistic company profiles with name, industry, department, job titles, website, and slogan. Perfect for populating B2B demos and testing.
company.feature1Desc: Company name, industry, department, job titles, website, email, phone, and slogan.
company.feature2Desc: Company names follow real-world patterns including legal forms (Inc, LLC, Ltd) and naming conventions.
company.feature3Desc: Support for 15+ industries with appropriate job titles and department structures.
company.feature4Desc: Generate unlimited company profiles instantly with no sign-up required.
company.faq_q1: Are companies based on real businesses?
company.faq_a1: No. All company profiles are randomly generated and do not represent real businesses.
company.faq_a2: Yes. You can select specific industries or generate random companies across all industries.
company.faq_a3: Yes. Copy individual profiles or export in bulk as TXT, CSV, or JSON formats.

credential.heroDesc: Generate secure passwords, memorable passphrases, PIN codes, API keys, JWT secrets, UUIDs, and bulk credential pairs. All generation happens in your browser.
credential.feature1Desc: Random passwords, passphrases, PINs, API keys, JWT secrets, UUIDs, and credential pairs.
credential.feature2Desc: Configurable character types, entropy indicators, and strength meters for every credential.
credential.feature3Desc: Generate multiple credentials at once. Perfect for seeding databases and user testing.
credential.feature4Desc: All generation happens in your browser. Nothing is sent to any server.
credential.faq_a1: Yes. Passwords are generated with configurable character sets and include entropy indicators.
credential.faq_a2: All generation happens entirely in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
credential.faq_a3: Yes. You can generate multiple credentials at once and copy or export them in various formats.

barcode.heroDesc: Generate EAN-13, UPC, Code 128, ISBN, GTIN and dozens of other barcode formats. Check digits, validator, bulk generation, and SVG/PNG export — all free and client-side.
barcode.feature1Desc: EAN-13, UPC-A, Code 128, Code 39, ISBN, ISSN, ITF-14, GS1-128, and more.
barcode.feature2Desc: Automatic check digit calculation with step-by-step explanations for all supported standards.
barcode.feature3Desc: Generate up to 1000 barcodes at once. Export as CSV, JSON, or TXT.
barcode.feature4Desc: Export individual barcodes as scalable SVG or high-resolution PNG for printing.
barcode.faq_a1: Yes. All barcodes have correct check digits and follow encoding rules. They will scan correctly with any compatible reader.
barcode.faq_a2: No. Everything runs in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
barcode.faq_a3: Yes. Export as SVG for professional print quality or PNG for quick use. SVGs scale without resolution loss.

phoneGenerator.pageDesc: Select a country and generate valid phone numbers for 245+ countries and regions worldwide. All numbers pass libphonenumber validation and support multiple formats.

hero.title: Generate Secure Credentials
hero.subtitle: Generate passwords, passphrases, API keys, UUIDs, JWT secrets, random tokens and developer credentials — all locally in your browser.

credentialLanding.audience_desc_developers: Generate UUIDs, JWT secrets, API keys, webhook secrets, and session tokens for your applications.
credentialLanding.audience_desc_qa: Create bulk test credentials, export to CSV/JSON, and generate unique test accounts for your test suites.
credentialLanding.audience_desc_devops: Generate secrets, random tokens, Docker secrets, database passwords, and SSH keys for your infrastructure.
credentialLanding.audience_desc_everyone: Create strong passwords, memorable passphrases, PIN codes, and Wi-Fi passwords for personal use.
credentialLanding.tools_password_desc: Secure random passwords with full control
credentialLanding.tools_pin_desc: Numeric PIN codes of any length
credentialLanding.tools_analyzer_desc: Check strength, entropy, and crack time
credentialLanding.security_crypto_desc: Cryptographically secure random values from the Web Crypto API
credentialLanding.security_browser_desc: All generation happens in your browser — no data sent to any server
credentialLanding.security_tracking_desc: We do not track what you generate. No analytics on generated data
credentialLanding.security_storage_desc: Generated credentials are never stored on any server or cloud
credentialLanding.security_history_desc: History is stored only in your browser’s localStorage — you control it
credentialLanding.security_opensource_desc: Full source code is available on GitHub for audit and transparency
credentialLanding.learn_password_vs_passphrase_desc: Why word sequences can be both stronger and more memorable
credentialLanding.learn_entropy_desc: Understanding bits of entropy and what they mean for security
credentialLanding.learn_password_length_desc: Minimum lengths by use case and threat model
credentialLanding.learn_what_is_uuid_desc: Universally Unique Identifiers and their variants
credentialLanding.learn_uuid_v4_vs_v7_desc: Random vs time-ordered — when to use each
credentialLanding.learn_what_is_jwt_secret_desc: HMAC secrets for signing JSON Web Tokens
credentialLanding.learn_api_keys_desc: Prefix-based, bearer tokens, and security best practices
credentialLanding.learn_base64_desc: Binary-to-text encoding and when to use Base64URL
credentialLanding.learn_crypto_randomness_desc: Why Math.random() is not enough for security

faqs.a: All passwords are generated using crypto.getRandomValues(), the Web Crypto API’s cryptographically secure pseudorandom number generator. This is the same standard used by browsers for security-sensitive operations.
Yes. All generation happens entirely in your browser. No data is sent to any server, and no credentials ever leave your device.
No. Generated passwords are never stored on our servers. You can optionally save them to your browser’s localStorage (history feature), which stays on your device.

faqs.q: What password length should I use?
faqs.a: For most purposes, 16 characters provides excellent security. For master passwords or admin accounts, use 20-32 characters. Wi-Fi passwords should be 63 characters for maximum WPA2/WPA3 compatibility.

Entropy measures how unpredictable a password is, expressed in bits. Each bit doubles the difficulty of guessing. A password with 80+ bits of entropy is considered very strong against offline attacks.
A passphrase is a sequence of random words (e.g., “correct-horse-battery-staple”). Passphrases are easier to remember than random character passwords while providing comparable or better security.
A UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is a 128-bit label used for unique identification. UUID v4 is randomly generated, while UUID v7 includes a timestamp component for time-ordered sorting.
A JWT secret is a cryptographic key used to sign JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) via HMAC algorithms like HS256. It should be a high-entropy random string, typically 64 bytes encoded in Base64URL.
